title: The Macro Minute: Did Fed Chair Powell just rubberstamp Paradigm C?

Happy Wednesday out there, team 42. Your
skipper here, Darius Delta, present our
macro minute for Wednesday, October
15th, 2025. Hope everyone's having a
great week. So, as always, we'll start
with the executive summary from today's
good old morning note. If you would like
to review the analysis supporting these
conclusions and to see if you need to
make any changes in your portfolio to
remain on the right side of market risk
or to get on the right side of market
risk to the extent you haven't followed
KISS or Dr. Mo, you need to be a client
of 42 Macro. So, today's key macro
question is did Fed chair Powell just
rubber-stamp paradigm C? Our answer? Not
quite, but he took a significant step in
that direction yesterday. Speaking at
the National Association for Business
Economics annual meeting on Tuesday,
Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell
indicated that the US Central Bank is
preparing to implement another 25 basis
point rate cut later this month and
suggested the Fed may soon end balance
sheet run-off to safeguard liquidity in
overnight funding markets.
In support of his incrementally dovish
forward guidance, Powell repeatedly
highlighted sluggish hiring trends and
warned of further deterioration ahead.
Skipping this uh this these two
paragraphs here cuz they're very
important. Quantitative risk management
signals that are paying members
pay to access.
Skipping ahead. Uh while we're not of
the view that more financial repression
and monetary debasement will end well
for consumers, businesses, and sectors
gasping for economic oxygen at the
bottom of the K-shaped US economy, we
are of the view that creating the
illusion of growth via faster nominal
GDP, which is paradigm C and paradigm B
in the print phase, is better for the
median household than keeping half or
two-thirds of the economy in recession
for 3-plus years just to chase an
arbitrary 2% inflation target.
To be clear, paradigm B, which is the
cut phase of the cut-grow-print phase
that required to address the
geopolitically driven supply-demand
imbalance in the Treasury bond market
that we identified back in summer 2023
that is obviously had uh quite a big
market impact if you look at price of
stocks, gold, and Bitcoin since then.
Uh to be clear, paradigm B is the best
outcome from a long-term perspective
because it would create the conditions
required for a durable expansion of real
median income. That said, the elevated
political volatility of this foreturning
all but ensures that taking short-term
pain for long-term gain will not occur
over any reasonable time horizon.
Our hope is that there is some real
median income growth in paradigm C
before paradigm D ultimately arrives,
likely in 5 to 10 years. Check with us
for in terms of our Bayesian inference
process uh on that. That's not a static
forecast. We will plant the flag and say
paradigm C is over when it's over in
terms of our Bayesian research process,
and we will plant the flag in terms of
the fiscal the monetary policy response
to the end of paradigm C as the
beginning of paradigm D. Again, I'm
assuming that's three, four, five years
from now, who knows? I have no idea.
It's my job to wake up every day,
refresh our quantitative risk management
signals, piece together the macro puzzle
with the with the with the new data and
the new policy guidance that we're
getting in terms of trying to figure out
the fundamental themes and refresh and
repeat that six days a week for our
members of team 42 here. Uh so,
transition to our 42 Macro dashboard. As
always, we'll wrap up with a question
from our community. Uh this one's uh not
really a question, but I I thought it
was important to highlight in the
context of everything that's happening
in markets it recently in terms of the
stress that some folks are feeling here
at what some believe is uh the peak of a
bubble. So, uh this one's titled real
results with 0% downside capture in
stress.

to express my appreciation for your work
and the clarity you bring to the
process. I recently noticed that my
money-weighted weight of
rate of return in my Fidelity brokerage
account has been noticeably hot
noticeably higher than my time-weighted
rate of return. Clear numerical proof
that your guidance and market timing are
generating real alpha. That's really
impressive.
It's a lot harder to have a higher uh
money-weighted rate of return than
time-weighted rate of return cuz you're
obviously starting with a lower
comparative base in the time-weighted
rate of return. So, that means this
person, as they make more money, is
making more money faster uh partially as
a function, if not largely as a
function, of our uh research and risk
management signals. Uh beyond the
numbers, the stress-free discipline of
following KISS and Dr. Mo
dispassionately
has been invaluable.
It's one thing to see results on paper,
but another to experience the calm
confidence that comes from a clear
framework.
That combination is worth its weight in
gold, which is coincidentally the title
of a book that's featured about me, um
available on Amazon. Uh
that was written about me. Uh appreciate
all that you do, your research, your
integrity, and conviction behind your
process. It's making a measurable
difference. Best regards. So, really
appreciate this. This is uh
this is why we built the company.
These this type of feedback is why we
built the company. You know, I I tweeted
yesterday, you know, partially
tongue-in-cheek, but partially true,
you know, we built this company so that
Main Street investors and Wall Street
investors, by the way, we have, you
know,
we have institutional clients that
manage a collective 25-plus trillion
dollars in assets. So, it's not really
leaving them in the dust, but I left
that ecosystem, or at least took a step
out of that ecosystem, one foot out of
the door, to build this, to bring these
people into the ecosystem.
You shouldn't have to pay 1% of your
money a year
to some financial advisor who's on a
golf course on a beautiful day like this
trying to secure new business instead of
managing risk in your portfolio
to achieve stellar results.
You shouldn't have to pay,
you know, earmuffs, Wall Street clients,
two and 20
to achieve three and 30 type results
from an absolute return and
risk-adjusted return perspective.
My goal with this company, as it always
has been and always will be, is to make
sure that everybody we service, whether
they be the top-flight hedge fund or
asset manager across global Wall Street
or whether they be somebody with a few
thousand bucks in their retail brokerage
account, I don't care who they are. I
just want to level the playing field.
I'm someone who believes in the E-shaped
economy, not the K-shaped economy.
People don't have to lose for others to
win. We can all win together. And that's
what 42 Macro is proving daily in a
nutshell. So, we're really grateful for
this feedback. Thank you.
Thank you guys all for tuning in. We'll
catch you back here tomorrow. Have a
great day out there. Cheers.
